title: Therapy Support Associate
description:
Job Description

  • Assist in fulfilling the mission and vision of St Luke's ElderCare.
  • Assist the therapists in physical treatments and administrative duties.

Clinical Duties

  • To follow & maintain the standard of clinical practice
  • To assist in implementing prescribed exercise programme and conducting group therapy
  • To feedback on patient's progress (reporting)
  • To communicate with patient at professional level
  • To communicate with therapist at professional level
  • To attend to two patients simultaneously if required
  • To be responsible for coverage of service to assigned ward(s) or out-patient unit

Non-Clinical Aspects

  • To be able to work in the Rehabilitation Team and provide feedback on department process and operation issues
  • To coordinate matters related to equipment maintenance & repair (external)
  • To assist in maintenance and repair of exercise equipment
  • To liaise with Support Service Department for equipment maintenance (internal)
  • To maintain equipment on the ward trolleys (if applicable)
  • To ensure tidiness and cleanliness of all therapy areas (PT gym, OT ADL area & UL room)
  • To report on the loss or damage of any rehab equipment

Administrative Duties

  • To maintain department library
  • To maintain department inventory
  • To provide administrative duties e.g. filling document, assists in statistics.
  • To help out in photocopy & dispatch of documents
  • To be responsible for daily billing as applicable
  • To participate in departmental meetings
  • To replenish supply of assessment forms & exercise handouts

Education and Requirements

  • To participate in basic therapy assistant training course
  • To participate in ongoing education or upgrading program for therapy assistants
  • To be involved in orientation program for new therapy assistants
  • To participate in supervision and mentoring program for therapy assistants
  • Degree/Diploma in Physiotherapy/Occupational Therapy from a recognised university
  • Preferably with relevant therapy working experience.

postedCompany.description:
St Luke’s ElderCare (SLEC) is a Christian healthcare provider committed to meeting the evolving needs of the community, excelling in social care and clinical excellence for ageing well. Our ecosystem of care comprises centre, community, residential and home-based services.

SLEC’s range of centre-based services includes dementia day care, maintenance day care, and community rehabilitation including pulmonary, musculoskeletal, physiotherapy, speech, and occupational therapy. Community-based services offer active ageing and wellness programmes, diabetic screenings, dietetic services, and nursing care. Home-based services provide dietetic, medical, nursing, and therapy services. Residential-based services provide long-term care for those that require daily living and nursing care.

We operate Senior Care and Rehabilitation Centres, Active Ageing Centres (Care) and Residences (nursing homes), serving mainly older persons regardless of race, language and religion.

Incorporated in 1999, SLEC is a registered charity and an Institution of Public Character.
postedCompany.name: ST LUKE'S ELDERCARE LTD.
